identity.canonical_title: Start Time TIMESTAMP Time TIMESTAMP Trad Relation

theory.canonical_statement: ID4650 records Start Time TIMESTAMP Time TIMESTAMP Trad Relation as a source-extracted canonical equation.
theory.technical_interpretation: The entry preserves the equation exactly as extracted from `0_Incoming\Manual_Uploads\TXT_20260224_182616\yes thats  great lets please do, -153a4e1ae6baa2aa.txt` for registry alignment and later derivation.

latex_blocks: $$ DECLARE start_time TIMESTAMP; end_time TIMESTAMP; trad_time INTERVAL; daans_time INTERVAL; test_point GEOGRAPHY; i INTEGER; BEGIN -- Create a test point in SF test_point := ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(-122.4194, 37.7749), 4326)::geography; -- Benchmark traditional PostGIS transformation start_time := clock_timestamp(); FOR i IN 1..iterations LOOP -- What Google does now: multiple trigonometric operations PERFORM ST_Transform(test_point::geometry, 3857); -- Web Mercator PERFORM ST_Distance(test_point, test_point); PERFORM ST_Area(ST_Buffer(test_point, 100)); END LOOP; end_time := clock_timestamp(); trad_time := end_time - start_time; -- Benchmark DAANSphere transformation start_time := clock_t ...
canonical_equation.source_section_latex: $$ DECLARE start_time TIMESTAMP; end_time TIMESTAMP; trad_time INTERVAL; daans_time INTERVAL; test_point GEOGRAPHY; i INTEGER; BEGIN -- Create a test point in SF test_point := ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(-122.4194, 37.7749), 4326)::geography; -- Benchmark traditional PostGIS transformation start_time := clock_timestamp(); FOR i IN 1..iterations LOOP -- What Google does now: multiple trigonometric operations PERFORM ST_Transform(test_point::geometry, 3857); -- Web Mercator PERFORM ST_Distance(test_point, test_point); PERFORM ST_Area(ST_Buffer(test_point, 100)); END LOOP; end_time := clock_timestamp(); trad_time := end_time - start_time; -- Benchmark DAANSphere transformation start_time := clock_t ...

blocks.latex: $$ DECLARE start_time TIMESTAMP; end_time TIMESTAMP; trad_time INTERVAL; daans_time INTERVAL; test_point GEOGRAPHY; i INTEGER; BEGIN -- Create a test point in SF test_point := ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(-122.4194, 37.7749), 4326)::geography; -- Benchmark traditional PostGIS transformation start_time := clock_timestamp(); FOR i IN 1..iterations LOOP -- What Google does now: multiple trigonometric operations PERFORM ST_Transform(test_point::geometry, 3857); -- Web Mercator PERFORM ST_Distance(test_point, test_point); PERFORM ST_Area(ST_Buffer(test_point, 100)); END LOOP; end_time := clock_timestamp(); trad_time := end_time - start_time; -- Benchmark DAANSphere transformation start_time := clock_t ...
